title: btrfs: honor btrfs.size from storage.conf
body:
**Summary**
Fixes the bug where [storage.options.btrfs] size in storage.conf produced unknown option btrfs.size (or equivalent) because the btrfs graph driver’s parseOptions did not handle the size key while pkg/config already emits `btrfs.size=...`

**What changed**
- `parseOptions`: accept size the same way as `min_space` (units.RAMInBytes, enable quota when set) so `btrfs.size=...` from config is accepted at driver init.
-  `Create / CreateReadWrite` delegate to internal `create(..., applyDriverDefaultQuota bool)`. For read-write layers, when there is no explicit `storage-opt` size, the driver default from config is applied inside create in bytes, without mutating the caller’s `StorageOpt`
- `btrfs_test.go`: unit test for `btrfs.size=...` parsing.


How to verify
- `storage.conf`:

```
[storage]
driver = "btrfs"

[storage.options.btrfs]
size = "5g"
```
- Storage init should succeed; RW layers should get the default quota when no per-container `size` is set.

Fixes: [#718](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/718)
